notes: Official stowage examination — basic fee for vessels up to 24,999 DWT. Covers pre-loading hold cleanliness inspection.
Official stowage examination — vessels 25,000-49,999 DWT.
Official stowage examination — vessels 50,000-74,999 DWT.
Official stowage examination — vessels 75,000-99,999 DWT.
Official stowage examination — vessels 100,000-149,999 DWT.
Official stowage examination — vessels 150,000+ DWT (Capesize and above).
Hourly rate for official inspection services beyond scheduled stowage examination. Applies to re-inspections after failed holds.
Surcharge applied when holds fail initial inspection and require re-examination. Per reinspection event.
Review and acceptance of fumigation certificate when fumigation required.
Official inspection of cargo samples — per sample drawn.
NCB Certificate of Readiness — grain code compliance. Vessels under 25,000 DWT. Includes structural, stability, cleanliness review.
NCB Certificate of Readiness — vessels 25,000-49,999 DWT.
NCB Certificate of Readiness — vessels 50,000-74,999 DWT (Supramax/Ultramax range).
NCB Certificate of Readiness — vessels 75,000-99,999 DWT (Panamax range).
NCB Certificate of Readiness — vessels 100,000-149,999 DWT (Post-Panamax).
NCB Certificate of Readiness — Capesize and above.
NCB review of grain loading stability calculations. Required if no current DOA on file.
USDA cleanliness certificate issuance per hold. Typically done hold-by-hold at larger terminals.
Overtime surcharge for inspections outside normal business hours (evenings, weekends, holidays).
Holiday overtime rate for FGIS inspection services.
